title: 10 Facts About Payment Gateways for Cameroon Digital Products That Change How You Sell
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: Selling digital products from Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Pakistan, or Bangladesh is possible with crypto-native infrastructure that has no geographic restrictions.

content.text: Selling digital products from Cameroon? Traditional payment gateways like PayPal and Stripe often block creators in your region. Geographic restrictions, banking hurdles, and KYC requirements lock you out of global markets. But there’s a solution: blockchain-based payments. This list breaks down 10 facts about payment gateways tailored for Cameroon’s digital creators. Learn how to bypass restrictions, accept payments globally, and automate delivery without a bank account. Whether you sell ebooks, courses, or art, these facts will change how you operate.
The Full List With Context

1. **No KYC Required**: Blockchain gateways like NOWPayments let you accept payments without identity verification. 2. **300+ Coins Supported**: Buyers can pay with BTC, ETH, USDT, SOL, and more. 3. **Instant Automated Delivery**: Files deliver automatically once payment is verified. 4. **Global Reach**: Sell to anyone, anywhere, without geographic restrictions. 5. **No Bank Account Needed**: Bypass traditional banking hurdles. 6. **One-Time Payment**: Lifetime access to tools like Unchained Commerce’s Core Shop Engine ($49.99). 7. **Multi-Chain QR Codes**: Simplify payments with frontend wallet integration (Web3 Wallet Connect, $19.99). 8. **Live Price Feeds**: Display real-time prices in any currency. 9. **PDF Receipts**: Automate professional receipts for every transaction. 10. **Lifetime Access**: No subscriptions—pay once, own forever.
Why Each Item on This List Matters

KYC-free payments remove barriers for creators in restricted regions. Supporting 300+ coins means more buyers can pay in their preferred currency. Instant delivery builds trust and reduces support requests. Global reach opens markets beyond Cameroon. No bank account required means freelancers and creators without access to traditional banking can still sell. One-time payments eliminate recurring costs. Multi-chain QR codes streamline the checkout process. Live price feeds reduce confusion over currency conversions. PDF receipts add professionalism. Lifetime access ensures long-term value without hidden fees.
The Ones Most Creators Get Wrong

Many creators assume they need a PayPal or Stripe account to sell globally. Wrong. Blockchain gateways like NOWPayments work without them. Others think setting up crypto payments is complicated. With tools like Web3 Wallet Connect ($19.99), it’s plug-and-play. Some believe they need a bank account to receive payments. Not true—crypto wallets suffice. Lastly, creators often overlook automated delivery, leading to manual work and delays. Instant delivery is non-negotiable for scaling.
Blockchain payments remove geographic barriers, letting Cameroon creators sell globally.
How to Prioritise What to Implement First

Creators in Ethiopia, Cameroon, Tanzania, Venezuela, Iran, and the Philippines face a specific challenge: not only are conventional payment platforms unavailable or severely limited, but the local banking infrastructure may add additional friction to receiving international transfers even through alternative channels. Crypto payments remove this layer entirely. A seller in Addis Ababa and a seller in Amsterdam receive USDT transactions through the same infrastructure, at the same speed, with the same fees. The blockchain has no knowledge of which country a wallet is located in. This geographic neutrality is the core property that makes crypto-native infrastructure the right foundation for creators in restricted markets.
Putting the List Into Action

Choose a blockchain payment gateway like NOWPayments. Integrate frontend wallet connectivity with Web3 Wallet Connect ($19.99) for seamless payments. Set up automated delivery using Unchained Commerce’s Core Shop Engine ($49.99). Test the system with a small audience. Launch globally, targeting markets beyond Cameroon. Monitor transactions with an admin dashboard ($29.99) for real-time insights. Scale by adding more products and currencies.
